name: edge: open a large file (1 MB) without hanging
comment: Opens a large (1 MB, ~19k lines) plain-text file alongside a second normal file in one launch. tryToBreakIt: a large document must open as a tab without crashing or hanging - the window comes up, the editor is present, and typing afterward still lands (app stays interactive). 1 MB is the bounded 'large' size: it exercises the big-document load path (synchronous text load + full-range attribute pass) and reliably completes within the timeout. medit's file open is synchronous on the main thread, so open time scales with size; a VERY large file (5 MB+) batched with another file stalls window creation past any reasonable timeout - that is a tracked medit perf defect (docs/autopilot-feedback.md, medit defect M1: large-file open should be incremental/off-main-thread), deliberately NOT asserted here so this plan stays green while the defect is open. Documented primitives only. Requires the Debug build; stage fixtures first (generates the 1 MB large file + zero-byte partner).
